question: How to use semantic HTML5 elements
description: Replace generic <div> containers with semantic elements like <main>, <article>, <section>, and <aside> — improving accessibility, SEO, and code readability.

steps.title: Page-level structure: header, main, footer
steps.explanation: <header> contains the logo and navigation. <main> wraps the page's primary content — there should only be one per page. <footer> holds legal, contact, and site-wide links.

steps.title: Content sectioning: article, section, aside
steps.explanation: <article> is self-contained content that makes sense on its own (blog post, card, tweet). <section> groups thematically related content inside an article or page. <aside> holds supplementary content tangentially related to the main content.

steps.title: Inline semantics: time, mark, figure, address
steps.explanation: Inline semantic elements mark up meaning within a paragraph. <time> marks dates and times with a machine-readable datetime. <mark> highlights text. <figure> wraps images, diagrams, and code examples with an optional <figcaption>.

steps.title: Navigation patterns
steps.explanation: Use <nav> for landmark navigation blocks. Provide aria-label to distinguish multiple <nav> elements on one page (e.g. main navigation vs. breadcrumb vs. pagination).
